user_query:
I want a dry-run plan for post-merge branch clean-up. Read input/branches.json and simulate the following policy:
- For every branch listed under merged_into_main, plan to rename it by appending "--merged-YYYY-MM-DD".
- The date must be the merge date from merge_dates if present; otherwise fall back to the branch tip date from branch_tip_dates. If neither date exists for a merged branch, skip it with reason "could not determine merge date".
- Never include main or master. Skip these with reason "main/master" if they appear in merged_into_main.
- Skip any branch whose name already contains "--merged-" with reason "already renamed".
- Skip the current_branch with reason "currently checked out".

Only consider branches listed in merged_into_main as candidates; do not propose renames for any other local_branches. Do not assume any git state beyond what is in the file.

Write three outputs under output/:
1) output/rename_plan.json — a JSON array of rename operations sorted by the original branch name ascending. Each object must include exactly:
   - branch: the original branch name
   - new_name: the proposed new name with the appended suffix
   - used_date: the date string used (YYYY-MM-DD)
   - date_source: either "merge" or "tip"
2) output/skipped.json — a JSON array of skipped branches sorted by branch name ascending. Each object must include exactly:
   - branch
   - reason — one of: "main/master", "already renamed", "currently checked out", "could not determine merge date"
3) output/summary.txt — plain text with:
   - line 1: "renamed: X" where X is the count of rename operations
   - line 2: "skipped: Y" where Y is the count of skipped branches
   - subsequent lines: one per planned rename in the format "BRANCH -> NEW_NAME", sorted by BRANCH ascending.

Use only the data provided in input/branches.json. Do not include any branches outside merged_into_main. Ensure exact field names, reason strings, and sorting.
